welcome.title: Writing Studio
welcome.body: A dockable workspace for this book. Panels (compose, planner, cast, quality…) will be added here one at a time. Drag tabs to split, stack, float, or pop out into their own window.
welcome.paletteHint: ⌘⇧P also opens the Command Palette anytime

arc-templates.title: Arc Templates
arc-templates.desc: Browse, create, adopt and apply reusable multi-chapter arc structures — parallel plot threads and cast roles over a chapter span
arc-templates.guideBody: The Arc Template library holds reusable multi-chapter structures — parallel plot threads (revenge line, romance, mentor's lie) laid out as motifs across a chapter span, with cast roles the arc binds when you apply it. Browse by tier: your own templates, the system-seeded ones, and (in the Catalog) others' public ones. Create a new template, adopt a system/public one to customize your own copy, or open one to see its thread × chapter timeline and preview applying it to this book. You can also save an arc you have already authored as a template, or import a reference story and deconstruct it (拆文) into a new template. Applying a template is a preview first — it never overwrites your chapters without you materializing it.

arc-inspector.title: Arc Inspector
arc-inspector.desc: Read and edit ONE arc or saga — its goal, status, the cascade-resolved plot tracks and cast roster, chapter span, open promises, and template provenance
arc-inspector.guideBody: The Arc Inspector opens one node of your book's spec tree — a saga, an arc, or a sub-arc. This tree is not decoration: its plot tracks and cast bindings are read into every generation prompt, so editing an arc here changes how the AI drafts its chapters. Tracks and roster slots resolve as a cascade — an entry inherited from a parent saga is shown greyed with an Override action (editing it in place would silently fork a copy). The chapter span, open-promise rollup, and template provenance are read-only. Archiving an arc returns its chapters to the unplanned tray; restoring re-attaches them. Open it from the Plan Hub (select an arc), the command palette, or the agent.

body.selectArc: Select an arc to see its plan.
body.archivedBanner: Archived — its chapters returned to the unplanned tray. Restore to edit and re-attach them.
body.archivingNote: Archiving returns its chapters to the unplanned tray; restoring re-attaches them.

compose.title: Co-writer Chat
compose.guideBody: The AI co-writer for this book — chat with it about a scene, ask it to draft or revise a passage, or brainstorm what happens next. Open it when you want a conversational partner that already knows this book's characters, glossary, and prior chapters.

scene-compose.title: Scene Compose
scene-compose.desc: Draft a scene with AI candidates
scene-compose.guideBody: Draft a scene with the AI co-writer: pick a scene and a model, then Generate to stream a ghost draft — or turn on Diverge to get several candidates you can compare, edit, and accept. Accepting inserts the prose into the Editor for this chapter, and your regenerate/reject/edit choices teach the model. Open it when you want to write the next scene, not just chat about it.

chapter-assemble.title: Chapter Assemble
chapter-assemble.desc: Assemble or stitch scenes into a chapter
chapter-assemble.guideBody: Turn a chapter's scenes into finished chapter prose: 'Generate chapter' drafts it in one pass, or 'Stitch' merges your completed scene drafts into a single flowing chapter. Review the editable preview, then Accept to insert it into the Editor — and your edit/regenerate/reject choices teach the model, the same correction flywheel as Scene Compose. Open it once a chapter's scenes are drafted and you want to assemble them.

editor.title: Editor
editor.guideBody: The main manuscript editor where you write and edit chapter text directly. Open it whenever you're drafting new prose or making direct line edits — it's the canvas everything else (Compose, Planner, Steering) feeds into.

planner.title: Planner
planner.guideBody: Lay out the novel's structure — arcs, chapters, beats, and how they connect — before or while you write. Open it when you need to see or rework the shape of the story, not just the sentence in front of you.

usage.title: Usage
usage.guideBody: Shows how much you're spending across every AI-powered action — translations, enrichment, chat, compose — broken down by tokens and requests over time. Open it when you want to check your current spend against your budget, spot which feature is burning the most credits, or review a specific request's cost after the fact.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.guideBody: Collects job completions, translation runs, enrichment passes, and system alerts in one place so you don't have to keep checking back on long-running work. Open it after kicking off a big job (like translating a chapter or running enrichment) to know the moment it finishes, or to catch anything that failed and needs attention.

settings.title: Settings
settings.guideBody: Manage your account, set your Chat & AI defaults (models, reasoning effort, tool authority, grounding, voice), connect your own AI providers (OpenAI, local models, etc.), and configure translation defaults like target language and glossary behavior. Open it before starting a new project to set things up, or any time you need to swap providers, update credentials, or change how the assistant behaves by default.

trash.title: Trash
trash.guideBody: Holds books, chapters, and other items you've deleted, keeping them recoverable instead of gone for good. Open it when you've deleted something by mistake and need to restore it, or when you want to permanently clear out old items you no longer need.

steering.title: Steering
steering.desc: Author rules that guide AI on this book
steering.guideBody: Write the rules and preferences that guide how the AI writes for this specific book — tone, POV, things to avoid, recurring instructions you don't want to repeat every chat. Open it once early on, then revisit whenever the AI's output drifts from what you want.

style-voice.title: Style & Voice
style-voice.desc: Steer prose density and pace, and the distinct voice of each character — folded into every draft prompt
style-voice.guideBody: Style & Voice steers how the AI writes. Set the prose density and pace for the whole book (or narrow to a chapter or scene), and give each character a distinct speaking voice. These settings are folded into every draft prompt, so the writing stays consistent without you repeating instructions. Each control shows its effective value and where that value comes from (book default, or a chapter/scene override). Adjust it early to set the house style, then tweak per character as their voices emerge.
style-voice.noWork: No writing project yet — set up a Work first, then steer its style & voice here.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.desc: Entities, search, and bulk actions for this book
glossary.guideBody: Your book's living dictionary of characters, places, items, and other named entities — search, filter, and bulk-edit them from one place. Open it when you need to check what's already been established, fix a name across many entries at once, or clean up entities in bulk.

glossary-ontology.title: Ontology
glossary-ontology.desc: Kinds, genres & attributes for this book
glossary-ontology.guideBody: Defines the kinds (character, location, item, etc.), genres, and custom attributes available for classifying entities in this book. Open it before you start tagging entities, or whenever you need to add a new kind or attribute that doesn't fit the existing set.

glossary-unknown.title: Unknown entities
glossary-unknown.desc: Reassign unrecognized entities to a kind
glossary-unknown.guideBody: Lists entities the system spotted in your text but couldn't confidently classify into a kind. Open it periodically to assign these unrecognized names to the right kind so they show up correctly in the Glossary and Wiki instead of sitting in limbo.

glossary-ai-suggestions.title: AI suggestions
glossary-ai-suggestions.guideBody: Shows entity entries the AI drafted automatically from your manuscript, waiting for your review. Open it to accept, edit, or reject each suggestion before it becomes part of your official glossary.

glossary-merge-candidates.title: Merge candidates
glossary-merge-candidates.guideBody: Flags pairs of entities the system thinks might be duplicates (e.g. two spellings of the same character's name). Open it to confirm real duplicates and merge them, or dismiss false matches, keeping your glossary clean.

wiki.title: Wiki
wiki.desc: Browse and read this book's generated wiki articles
wiki.guideBody: The auto-generated wiki of readable articles built from your glossary and story content — one article per major entity. Open it when you want a narrative-style overview of a character, place, or faction instead of raw glossary fields.

wiki-editor.title: Wiki Editor
wiki-editor.desc: Edit a wiki article's prose, infobox, history and suggestions

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.desc: Browse and open your knowledge-graph projects
knowledge.guideBody: Knowledge is your entry point into a book's knowledge graph — the entities, events, and lore your AI agents have extracted and organized. Open it to pick which project (book) to work in before diving into entities, timelines, or the graph itself.

quality.title: Quality
quality.desc: Open promises, critic scores, story coverage & canon issues
quality.guideBody: Quality is your diagnostic dashboard — advisory only, nothing here changes your prose. Pick a card to see the open-promise debt ledger, per-chapter critic scores, whole-book promise coverage, or confirmed canon contradictions.

quality-promises.title: Promises
quality-promises.desc: The open-promise debt ledger (setups not yet paid off)
quality-promises.guideBody: Promises is your narrative debt ledger — every setup the story has made and not yet paid off. A promise opens where you plant it (a hinted betrayal, a loaded gun, an unexplained scar) and closes where the manuscript delivers on it. Work the list down before you finish a book; anything still open at the end is a thread readers will notice you dropped.

quality-critic.title: Critic
quality-critic.desc: Per-chapter coherence, voice, pacing & canon scores
quality-critic.guideBody: Critic scores a chapter on coherence, voice, pacing, and canon fidelity, with the reasoning behind each verdict. It is advisory — it never edits your prose. Use it to spot the chapter that drifted before a reader does, not as a grade to optimize.

quality-coverage.title: Story coverage
quality-coverage.desc: Whole-book audit of which outline promises got paid off
quality-coverage.guideBody: Story coverage is the whole-book view of the promise ledger: which outline beats the manuscript actually delivered, which are still outstanding, and where the payoffs cluster. Read it when a draft is complete to find the acts that promised more than they paid.

quality-canon.title: Canon issues
quality-canon.desc: Confirmed contradictions from generation & knowledge extraction
quality-canon.guideBody: Canon issues collects confirmed contradictions between your prose and your established lore — a character in two places at once, an eye colour that changed, a rule of magic the story just broke. Each issue links back to the chapter and the canon rule it violates, so you can fix the text or amend the rule.

quality-canon-rules.title: Canon rules
quality-canon-rules.desc: Author the invariants the critic enforces — create, edit, archive, restore
quality-canon-rules.guideBody: Canon rules is where you declare the invariants your story must never break — a character has one arm after chapter 41, spirit stones are consumed and never returned, the Sect Master's identity stays hidden until the Salt Court arc. These are the rules the critic and self-heal enforce on every generation, so writing them here is how you teach the co-writer your canon. It is the write half of Canon issues: that panel shows what broke, this one is where you fix the rule.

quality-corrections.title: Corrections
quality-corrections.desc: Your accept / edit / regenerate / reject rates on AI drafts — the quality signal
quality-corrections.guideBody: Corrections is the mirror on your own editing: every time you accept, edit, pick a different candidate, regenerate, or reject an AI draft, that choice is recorded — and this panel shows the rates, split by Diverge (K-option) vs Stream. A lower edit/regenerate/reject rate (and higher accept-as-is) in Diverge means the reranker is earning its extra latency. It is advisory and read-only; it never changes your prose — it tells you whether the co-writer is getting better at your book.

quality-heal.title: Self-heal
quality-heal.desc: Run Polish on a chapter and accept each fix before it touches your prose
quality-heal.guideBody: Self-heal runs a cheap proof-reading pass over a chapter and proposes fixes — pronoun drift, typos, canon slips, over-long descriptive beats — each shown as an accept/reject row. Deterministic fixes (typos, pronouns) come pre-ticked; semantic rewrites are unticked for you to judge. Nothing touches your prose until you press Apply, and even then it writes with a version check: if the chapter changed since Polish ran, the apply is refused rather than reverting your newer edits.

progress.title: Progress
progress.desc: Words today, your streak, the book total, a daily goal and a sparkline
progress.guideBody: Progress turns your writing into a habit you can see: words written today, your current day-streak, the book's running total, and a 7- or 30-day sparkline. Set a personal daily goal and a reference line marks it on the chart — the goal is yours alone, not shared with collaborators. It reads the word-counts the editor records as you save; it never changes your prose.

flywheel.title: Flywheel
flywheel.desc: What your last publish added to canon — new people, ties and events
flywheel.guideBody: Flywheel is the reward for publishing: when you publish a chapter, an extraction pass grows your world's knowledge, and this panel shows what it added — new entities, relations, and events — with clickable highlights that jump you straight to that character in Cast, that link in the graph, or that moment on the timeline. It closes the loop: writing feeds the canon, and the canon grounds your next chapter.

kg-overview.title: Overview
kg-overview.guideBody: Overview gives you a snapshot of a project's health at a glance — entity counts, recent activity, and pending work. Open it first when you return to a book to see what needs attention before drilling into a specific tool.

kg-entities.title: Entities
kg-entities.desc: Browse and search knowledge-graph entities, scoped to a project or across all your projects
kg-entities.guideBody: Entities lists every character, place, item, and concept the system has extracted, with search and filters to narrow by kind or project. Use it to look up a specific entity's details or to audit what the knowledge graph currently knows about your world.

kg-timeline.title: Timeline
kg-timeline.desc: Browse in-story events by chronological or narrative order
kg-timeline.guideBody: Timeline lays out the events of your story in order — either the sequence they happened in-world (chronological) or the sequence they were revealed to the reader (narrative). Open it to check consistency in your plot's sequencing or to see what's happened so far at any point in the story.

kg-evidence.title: Evidence
kg-evidence.desc: Search chapter and chat passages by semantic similarity
kg-evidence.guideBody: Evidence lets you search your chapters and chat history by meaning rather than exact wording, surfacing passages that discuss a topic even if they don't use the same phrasing. Use it when you need to find where something was mentioned or established, but can't remember the exact words.

kg-gap.title: Gap Report
kg-gap.desc: Find high-mention characters, places, and concepts missing from the glossary
kg-gap.guideBody: Gap Report surfaces characters, places, and concepts that come up often in your text but haven't been added to the glossary yet. Check it periodically to catch lore that's slipping through without a proper entry.

kg-proposals.title: Proposals
kg-proposals.desc: Review pending glossary, wiki, and lore-enrichment suggestions for this book
kg-proposals.guideBody: Proposals is where AI-suggested additions and changes to your glossary, wiki, and lore wait for your review. Open it to approve, edit, or reject each suggestion before it becomes part of your book's canon.

kg-schema.title: Schema
kg-schema.desc: Adopt, author, view, and sync your book's knowledge-graph schema
kg-schema.guideBody: Schema controls the structure of your book's knowledge graph — what kinds of entities and relationships it recognizes. Use it to adopt a starting schema, customize it for your world, or sync changes across your project.

kg-graph.title: Knowledge Graph
kg-graph.desc: Explore the project's entity relationship graph
kg-graph.guideBody: Knowledge Graph gives you a visual map of how entities in your project connect — who knows whom, what belongs where, which events involve which characters. Open it when you want to explore relationships spatially rather than as a list.

kg-insights.title: Mining Insights
kg-insights.desc: Extraction config quality, model performance & outcome drift across projects
kg-insights.guideBody: Mining Insights reports on how well extraction is performing across your projects — config quality, which models are producing good results, and where outcomes are drifting from expectations. Check it if extracted entities or events seem off, to diagnose whether the issue is the model or the config.

kg-jobs.title: Extraction Jobs
kg-jobs.desc: Monitor knowledge-extraction jobs across all your projects
kg-jobs.guideBody: Extraction Jobs shows the status of every knowledge-extraction run across all your projects, so you can see what's queued, in progress, done, or failed. Open it to check on long-running extractions or troubleshoot one that didn't finish as expected.

kg-bio.title: Global Bio
kg-bio.desc: Your cross-book author bio, used to ground AI writing and analysis
kg-bio.guideBody: Global Bio holds a short author bio that's shared across all your books, used to give AI writing and analysis tools context about your voice and background. Fill it in once and every project benefits from the same grounding.

kg-privacy.title: Privacy & Data
kg-privacy.desc: Export or permanently delete your knowledge-graph data
kg-privacy.guideBody: Privacy & Data lets you export a full copy of your knowledge-graph data or permanently delete it. Use it when you want a backup, are leaving the platform, or need to remove a project's data for good.

jobs-list.title: Jobs
jobs-list.guideBody: Shows the background jobs running for this book — translation runs, enrichment passes, exports, and other long-running tasks — along with their current status and progress. Open it to check whether a job is still running, see if one failed, or confirm a task finished before you move on.

job-detail.title: Job
job-detail.empty: Open a job from the Jobs panel to watch it here.

books.title: Books
books.desc: Browse and read your other books without leaving this studio
books.guideBody: Opens a lightweight library view of your other books right inside the studio, so you can check details, reread a passage, or jump between projects without losing your current editing session. Use it when you want quick cross-book reference without navigating away from what you're writing.

book-reader.title: Reader
book-reader.desc: Distraction-free reading view for a chapter

leaderboard-books.title: Leaderboard: Books
leaderboard-books.desc: Top-ranked books by rating, readers & favorites
leaderboard-books.guideBody: Shows which books are climbing the rankings by rating, reader count, and favorites, so you can see what's resonating with readers right now. Check this panel when you want a pulse on what's working across the platform, or to scout comparable titles in your genre.

leaderboard-authors.title: Leaderboard: Authors
leaderboard-authors.guideBody: Ranks authors by how many readers they've reached and how much they've published, giving you a sense of who's active and who's building an audience. Open it if you're curious how your output and reach compare, or looking for prolific authors to follow.

leaderboard-translators.title: Leaderboard: Translators
leaderboard-translators.guideBody: Ranks translators by chapters completed and number of languages covered, highlighting who's driving the platform's translation activity. Open this when you want to find active translators to collaborate with, or gauge how much translation work is happening across languages.

leaderboard-trending.title: Leaderboard: Trending
leaderboard-trending.guideBody: Surfaces the books gaining momentum right now, based on recent reader activity rather than all-time totals. Check it when you want to catch rising titles early or see what's currently capturing attention.

context-inspector.title: Context Inspector
context-inspector.desc: Trace what context management did per turn — budget, allocation, Planner→Compiler decisions
context-inspector.guideBody: Shows exactly what context was sent to the AI for a given turn — the token budget, what got included or dropped, and whether a quick pass or a fuller planning pass handled it. Open it when the AI's response seems off and you want to see what it actually "saw", or when you're curious how your context budget is being spent.

book-import.title: Import
book-import.desc: Import chapters from text/.docx/.epub, or a whole book from a PDF
book-import.intro: Bring existing manuscript content into this book.
book-import.guideBody: Import chapters from plain text, .docx, or .epub files, or a whole book from a PDF (with optional AI image captioning — you pick the vision model explicitly). Open it whenever you have existing content to bring in rather than writing from scratch.

pdf.title: Import from PDF
pdf.desc: Chunk a PDF into chapters, with optional AI captioning for images

scene-browser.title: Scene Browser
scene-browser.desc: Browse every scene in this book — the parsed prose and its authored plan, side by side
scene-browser.guideBody: The Scene Browser lists every scene across the whole book by joining two truths: the prose actually written (from book-service) and the authored intent/plan (from composition). A scene can be written-and-planned (normal), planned-but-not-yet-written, or written-but-not-yet-decompiled into a plan — so an imported book's scenes show here even before you make a plan.
scene-browser.noBook: Open a book to browse its scenes.
scene-browser.workless: No plan yet — scene intent (status, POV, tension) is unavailable until you create a plan for this book.
scene-browser.intentUnavailable: Scene plan is temporarily unavailable — showing the written prose only.
scene-browser.dirtyTitle: Canon moved since the last conformance run

plan-hub.title: Plan Hub
plan-hub.desc: The whole book's plan on a graph canvas — arc lanes, chapters, scenes and their links
plan-hub.guideBody: The Plan Hub renders your book's entire structure as a graph: each saga/arc/sub-arc is a lane, its chapters and scenes are cards along a story-order axis, and scene-links draw the causal edges between them. Open the panel to see every arc collapsed to a rollup card; expand an arc to lazily load its chapters, and a chapter to see its scenes. Pan and zoom to navigate; problem and staleness badges surface where canon or promises need attention.

decompose.title: Decompose
decompose.desc: Decompose your book against a story structure — preview the arc→chapter→scene tree, edit it, and commit
decompose.guideBody: Decompose turns a story structure into your book's outline. Pick one of your authored structures (or a built-in), add a premise, choose a model, then Preview: the AI proposes an arc→chapter→scene tree you can edit inline before committing. Commit writes the outline — your Manuscript tree updates to match. Open it from the Command Palette, or from a structure in the Structure Templates panel via "Use in decompose" (which pre-selects that structure here). No Work yet? The panel offers to set one up first.
decompose.loadError: Couldn't load this book's plan — try again.

plan-passes.title: Pass Rail
plan-passes.desc: Compile a plan pass-by-pass and approve each checkpoint
plan-passes.guideBody: The Pass Rail drives PlanForge's 7-pass compiler — motifs, cast, world, beats, character arcs, scenes, and self-heal — one pass at a time. Each pass shows whether it is fresh (its inputs are unchanged since it last ran) or stale, and the rail tells you how far the compiler can proceed on its own and which pass is waiting on you. Two passes are blocking checkpoints you must approve before the compiler moves past them: the cast (who your characters are) and the beats (what shape the story takes). Open a pass to read what it produced, edit it, then approve or hold. Editing a pass restales everything downstream automatically, so the plan can never drift out of sync with itself.

whatif-canvas.title: What-If Branches
whatif-canvas.desc: Explore alternate takes on a scene without committing — branch, generate an alt-take, compare, then promote the winner
whatif-canvas.guideBody: The what-if canvas lets you fork a scene into an ephemeral branch, generate an alternate take, and see the critic's judge badges side by side — all without touching the canonical draft. When a branch is the one you want, promote it: it becomes a real derivative work you can keep writing from. Nothing is saved until you promote, so you can explore freely. This is the editing surface behind the Promote button; the Plan Hub shows the plan graph, this is where you try alternatives on it.

divergence.title: Divergence (dị bản)
divergence.desc: Manage a book's what-if derivatives — list the canon and every branch, switch the whole studio to one, archive one, or spawn a new branch from a chapter
divergence.guideBody: A dị bản (divergence) branches your book at a chapter and diverges — an alternate universe, a POV shift, a character changed — into a derivative work with its own knowledge partition, while the source stays read-only canon. This panel lists your canonical work and every branch you've spawned. 'Switch to' moves your whole studio — editor, navigator, quality, everything — onto that branch; the choice is remembered per book and survives a reload. 'Archive' hides a branch without destroying it (its chapters and knowledge are kept). 'New divergence' opens the 4-step wizard to spawn one. Select a branch to read its spec (taxonomy, branch point, canon rules, overrides), which is fixed at creation.

reference-shelf.title: Reference shelf
reference-shelf.desc: Curate the reference passages (external influences) that ground the writing — add, edit, and remove them; per scene the relevant ones are retrieved and can be pinned into the grounding pack
reference-shelf.guideBody: The reference shelf is your book's corpus of influences — passages, sources, and style exemplars the AI draws on when it drafts. Add a reference with its title, author, source URL, and text; the text is embedded so the studio can retrieve the most relevant ones per scene. Edit a reference's details (a cheap metadata change) or its content (which re-embeds it). Filter a large shelf with the search box. Per-scene retrieval and pinning appear when you open the shelf alongside a scene; on its own it manages the library. An embedding model must be configured (add one from AI settings) for retrieval to work.
reference-shelf.noWork: No writing project yet — set up a Work first, then curate its reference shelf here.

canonview.title: Canon at chapter
canonview.desc: What canon knows as of the chapter in focus — entities present/established, canon state and timeline
canonview.guideBody: Answers 'what does canon know as of the chapter I'm on?' — the entities present in and established by the current chapter (from the glossary), plus the canon state (how many entities are active vs gone) and timeline events up to that point (from the knowledge graph). It follows the chapter you have in focus in the manuscript, and is the same inspector the what-if canvas shows at a branch point — so you can check what's already established before you diverge. Open a scene or chapter to give it a focus.

scene-inspector.title: Scene Inspector
scene-inspector.desc: Read and edit every field of one scene — intent, craft, tension and grounding
scene-inspector.guideBody: The Scene Inspector is the detail pane for a single scene: select a scene (in the Scene Browser or the manuscript) and its full plan opens here — status, goal, synopsis, beat role, tension, and the craft fields (conflict, outcome, stakes, story time, value shift, target words) — all editable, with grounding pins folded in. Edits save immediately and are guarded against conflicting changes.
scene-inspector.none: Select a scene to inspect its full plan.
scene-inspector.dirty: Canon moved since the last conformance run — this plan may be stale.

links.label: label
links.dup: A link of that kind already exists between these scenes.

chapter-browser.title: Chapter Browser
chapter-browser.desc: Sort, filter, search and bulk-act across this book's chapters
chapter-browser.bulk_not_wired: Coming soon — this bulk action isn't wired up yet
chapter-browser.client_filtered_note: Word-count/date filters apply to the currently loaded page
chapter-browser.guideBody: A sortable, filterable list of every chapter in the book, with search and bulk actions (like retagging or reordering several chapters at once). Open it when you need to find a specific chapter fast or make the same change across many chapters at once.

sharing.title: Sharing
sharing.desc: Visibility, unlisted link, and collaborators for this book
sharing.guideBody: Control who can see and edit this book — set it to private, generate an unlisted share link for anyone with the URL, or invite specific collaborators. Open this when you want to share a draft for feedback or hand off editing access without making the book public.

book-settings.title: Book Settings
book-settings.desc: Title, cover, genre tags, and world link for this book
book-settings.guideBody: Manage this book's core identity: its title, cover image, genre tags, and the world it's linked to. Open this when you're setting up a new book or need to update how it's labeled and categorized before sharing or publishing.

translation.title: Translation
translation.desc: Coverage matrix, language filter, bulk translate and glossary extraction for this book
translation.guideBody: Shows every language's translation progress for this book at a glance, with a coverage matrix and language filters so you can spot what's untranslated or stale. Open it to kick off bulk translation jobs or extract glossary terms discovered during translation, keeping terminology consistent across languages.

translation-versions.title: Translation Versions
translation-versions.desc: Per-chapter translation version management (compare, set active, re-translate)
translation-versions.empty: Open a chapter's translation versions from the Translation matrix.

translation-review.title: Translation Review
translation-review.desc: Block-aligned side-by-side review with per-block correction
translation-review.empty: Open a version's Review from the Translation Versions panel.

enrichment-compose.title: Create Enrichment
enrichment-compose.desc: Compose a new enriched lore draft (expand a draft, paste context, upload files, or state an intent)
enrichment-compose.guideBody: Use this panel to start a new lore draft when the wiki or glossary is missing an entry you need — expand a rough draft, paste in reference context, upload source files, or just describe your intent and let the AI generate a first pass. It's the entry point for turning "I need a bio for this character" into a structured, editable proposal.

enrichment-proposals.title: Enrichment Proposals
enrichment-proposals.desc: Review AI-proposed lore — approve, reject, or promote into canon
enrichment-proposals.guideBody: Every draft created in Compose (or generated by an enrichment job) lands here for review before it becomes canon — read through each proposed entry, approve it to promote it into the glossary, or reject it if it's wrong or unwanted. Open this panel whenever you want to check what the AI has drafted but hasn't yet been added to your book's official lore.

enrichment-gaps.title: Enrichment Gaps
enrichment-gaps.desc: Detect under-described entities and auto-enrich them as a background job
enrichment-gaps.guideBody: This panel scans your book for entities that are mentioned but under-described — characters, places, or items with too little detail in the glossary — and can queue them for automatic enrichment as a background job. Open it when you want to find and fill lore holes without manually hunting for what's missing.

enrichment-sources.title: Enrichment Sources
enrichment-sources.desc: License-tagged source corpora for retrieval and recook grounding
enrichment-sources.guideBody: Manage the source corpora (reference texts, uploaded documents, licensed material) that enrichment and recook jobs pull from for grounding, each tagged with its license so you know what's safe to reuse. Check here when you want to see what source material is feeding your AI-generated drafts, or to add new grounding material.

enrichment-jobs.title: Enrichment Jobs
enrichment-jobs.desc: Monitor and resume cost-capped enrichment jobs
enrichment-jobs.guideBody: Track the status of enrichment jobs you've kicked off — see what's running, completed, or paused, watch cost accumulate against the job's cap, and resume any job that stopped early. Open this panel when you want to check progress or pick back up a cost-capped enrichment run.

enrichment-settings.title: Enrichment Profile
enrichment-settings.desc: Author this book's de-bias profile (worldview, language, era, voice)
enrichment-settings.guideBody: Author this book's de-bias profile here — set the intended worldview, language conventions, era, and narrative voice so enrichment and compose drafts come out consistent with your book's tone instead of a generic default. Set this up early, before running enrichment jobs, so generated lore matches your book from the start.

json-editor.title: JSON
json-editor.desc: Raw JSON view of a resource

media-version-history.title: Version History
media-version-history.desc: Compare and restore past versions of an image or video block

original-source.title: Original Source
original-source.desc: The untranslated source text for this chapter
original-source.empty: Open a chapter from the Editor to view its original source.

extensions.title: Extensions
extensions.desc: Manage plugins, skills, MCP servers, commands & hooks
extensions.guideBody: Install and manage everything that adds capability to your agent — plugins, skills, MCP server connections, custom commands, and hooks. Open it when you want to add a new tool the agent can use, review what's currently enabled, or disable something that's misbehaving.

proposals.title: Proposals
proposals.guideBody: Lists skills the agent has suggested creating on its own, based on patterns it noticed in your work, so you can review and approve them before they become part of your toolkit. Open it after a session where the agent mentioned it could automate something, to decide whether to accept, edit, or reject the suggestion.

user-guide.title: User Guide
user-guide.desc: Every Studio tool, grouped by area — generated from the same catalog as the Command Palette
user-guide.guideBody: Lists every tool and panel in Writing Studio, grouped by area, with a plain-language explanation of what each one does. It's generated straight from the same catalog that powers the Command Palette and dock, so as new tools are added the guide updates automatically — it never falls out of sync with what's actually in the app.

agent-mode.title: Agent Mode
agent-mode.desc: Autonomous multi-chapter authoring runs — mission control
agent-mode.guideBody: Agent Mode is mission control for autonomous multi-chapter runs: the agent drafts straight through an approved plan while you watch. Start, pause, and resume a run from here, and review each chapter as it lands — its diff and its critic verdict — then accept, reject, or revert. Nothing it writes is final until you accept it.

chapter-revision-compare.title: Chapter Revision Compare
chapter-revision-compare.desc: Full editor diff for one chapter revision pair
chapter-revision-compare.empty: Open a chapter's revision diff from Agent Mode's review panel.

world-map.title: World Map
world-map.desc: Create and edit a world's reference maps — upload a base image, drop and drag location pins, draw and reshape regions.
world-map.guideBody: Author a world's raster maps. Upload a base image, then drop pins and draw regions on it. Drag a pin to move it (a drag is one absolute-coord PATCH on a stable marker id — never a delete-and-recreate) and drag a polygon vertex to reshape a region. Bind a pin or region to a glossary OR KG location entity (the picker labels the source). Every change saves to book-service; the assistant's world_map_* tools edit the same map live. Rename a map with optimistic-concurrency (If-Match), so a conflicting edit reloads instead of clobbering.

place-graph.title: Place Graph
place-graph.desc: The book's places (locations) as a draggable node graph
place-graph.guideBody: Add and connect the book's places (locations) as a node graph. Type a name and press + Place to author a location; toggle Link places, pick two nodes and a predicate (contains / borders / route_to) to connect them; drag to arrange (saved server-side, shared across your devices); set a backdrop image (needs an open chapter). Click a node to open it in the Cast codex. Locations only — use Author other kinds to add characters, items, and the rest.
place-graph.noWork: Set up the co-writer in Compose to arrange places.

cast.title: Cast
cast.desc: The book's cast — grouped, searchable, spoiler-safe; author & retire entities
cast.guideBody: Every character, place, organization and concept in this book's knowledge graph, grouped by kind and searchable, each with its spoiler-safe story-state (active/gone at your reading position). Create a new entity with + New, rename inline, edit aliases/kind, or retire (soft-archive) one. Click a row's arc glyph to open its Character Arc.

character-arc.title: Character Arc
character-arc.desc: One character's events on a timeline, the active→gone band, and 1-hop relations
character-arc.guideBody: One character's events in order on a compact arc, spoiler-cut at your reading position, with the active→gone state band and the current 1-hop relations. Pick a character, edit it, or add a relation with + link entity.

motif-library.title: Motif Library
motif-library.desc: Study tropes (套路/爽点/打脸), adopt or mine them, and inspect the motif relationship graph
motif-library.guideBody: The Motif Library is your narrative-craft workbench. Browse motifs by tier — your own, this book's labels, the shared book tier, the system seeds, the public catalog, and your mined drafts. Create a motif inline, adopt one from the catalog, or mine your own corpus to discover recurring patterns. Open any motif to read its beats and roles, and expand its graph to see what it is composed of, what it precedes, and its variants. Bind motifs to scenes from the Scene Inspector so the drafter actually writes toward them.

quality-conformance.title: Conformance
quality-conformance.desc: Beat-by-beat: did the prose realize the plan? Per-scene realized/not + tension band
quality-conformance.guideBody: Conformance traces, scene by scene, whether your written prose actually delivered the motif beats you planned for a chapter. Each scene shows realized / not-realized and how its tension compares to the target. It answers spec-vs-prose (not spec-vs-template — that is the arc-templates drift view). The signal is advisory (coarse, not causally verified). Regenerate a scene that missed its beat, or re-run the whole check against a model you pick.

motif-graph.title: Motif Graph
motif-graph.desc: The book-wide motif relationship graph as a draggable canvas — your + book-shared motifs and their composed-of / precedes / variant-of edges, with your own saved layout.
motif-graph.guideBody: A visual DAG of this book's motifs. Nodes are your motifs plus the book's shared-tier ones; edges are the composed_of / precedes / variant_of links. Drag a node to arrange the graph — your layout is saved per-book and follows you across devices (each collaborator has their own arrangement). The initial layout is auto-computed left-to-right along the precedes chains. Create and delete edges from a motif's detail in the Motif Library.
motif-graph.signedOut: Sign in to see the motif graph.
motif-graph.noBook: Open a book to see its motif graph.

structure-templates.title: Structure Templates
structure-templates.desc: Author custom story structures (a named ordered list of beats) and decompose your book against them — like the built-in Save the Cat or Hero's Journey
structure-templates.guideBody: Story structures are ordered beat sheets you decompose a book's chapters against. This panel lists the built-in structures (Save the Cat, Hero's Journey, Kishōtenketsu, Three-Act, Story Circle, Web-Novel Arc — read-only) plus your own. Clone a built-in to get an editable copy, then add, reorder, rename and remove its beats and save. Use a structure in Decompose to map its beats onto your chapters. Your custom structures are private to you and reusable across every book; archive one to hide it and restore it later. Built-ins stay intact for everyone — you always edit your own copy.

kg-triage.title: Triage
kg-triage.desc: Review extracted elements that didn't match your schema and resolve them.
kg-triage.guideBody: When extraction finds an entity kind, relationship, or value that isn't in your schema yet, it parks it here instead of guessing. Each row offers only the fixes that are valid for it — map it to something you already have, add it to your schema/vocabulary, fix an endpoint, or dismiss it.

search.title: Search
search.desc: Search your book's prose (text) or lore drawers (semantic); a hit opens the editor there.
search.guideBody: Search has two modes. Text finds exact words and phrases in your manuscript prose. Semantic finds passages by meaning across your lore drawers (chapters, chat, glossary). Click a result to open the editor at that chapter — search is a way into your manuscript, not a dead-end list.

workflows.title: Workflows
workflows.guideBody: Lists the multi-step workflow recipes your agent can run as a rail — the built-in System ones plus any you own. Open it to enable or disable a recipe for yourself, or delete one of your own. New recipes are added by approving a proposal in the Workflow Proposals panel, not created directly here.

workflow-proposals.title: Workflow Proposals
workflow-proposals.guideBody: Lists the workflow recipes the agent has suggested creating, based on multi-step sequences it noticed you repeat. Open it to approve a proposal — which mints the workflow so it becomes runnable — or reject it. This is where an agent-proposed workflow actually becomes real.

sceneRail.title: Scenes
sceneRail.empty: No scenes for this chapter (outline it in the composer).
sceneRail.notAnchored: Scene not anchored in the text yet — use ⚓ to link headings.
sceneRail.anchorNoop: {{count}} already anchored, {{unmatched}} without a matching heading.
sceneRail.anchorTitle: Link scenes to their headings in the text
sceneRail.addDisabled: Chapter has no outline yet (create it in the composer)

jsonEditor.empty: Open a resource as JSON from its panel ("Open as JSON").

planPasses.noBook: Open a book to compile its plan.
planPasses.noRun: No plan run yet — open the Planner to propose and compile one.
planPasses.notCompiled: This run has no compiled package yet. Compile it in the Planner before running passes.
planPasses.reviewPrompt: Approve {{pass}}? This lets the compiler proceed past it.
planPasses.seedApplied: Glossary seed applied — cast can be approved.
planPasses.seedGate: Your cast’s characters must be added to the glossary before you can approve. Apply the seed to add them.
planPasses.editEmpty: Nothing here yet — add a row.

planner.proposeBlind: Proposed from this braindump only. Existing chapters are not read.
planner.empty: Paste your novel-system markdown and Propose to start a plan run.

list.title: Runs for this book
list.empty: No plan runs yet for this book.

planner.grounded: Grounded on {{chapters}} existing chapter(s) + {{cast}} cast member(s); {{arcs}} existing arc(s) continued.
planner.groundOnExisting: Continue this book — ground on its existing cast, arcs & recent chapters

editor.empty: Select a chapter in the manuscript navigator to edit it here.
editor.derivativeForked: On a dị bản — this chapter is FORKED, isolated from canon. Edits save to the branch; canon stays untouched.
editor.derivativeInherit: On a dị bản — this chapter still mirrors canon. Editing here FORKS it into the branch (canon stays untouched).
editor.mergeDone: Merged into canon — the branch keeps its own version.
editor.mergeConflict: Canon changed since — reopen the chapter and merge again.

sceneCompose.empty: Select a chapter in the manuscript navigator to draft its scenes here.
sceneCompose.insertFailed: Could not insert — click into the chapter text in the Editor, then Accept again.
sceneCompose.openedEditor: Opened the Editor on this chapter — Generate again to insert the draft here.

chapterAssemble.empty: Select a chapter in the manuscript navigator to assemble it here.

popout.openTitle: Open Compose in its own window

steering.heading: Steering rules
steering.subheading: Persistent author guidance injected into AI turns for this book.
steering.noBook: Open this panel from a book to author its steering rules.
steering.capReached: Rule limit reached ({{max}}). Delete a rule to add another.
steering.empty: No steering rules yet. Add one to guide the AI on this book.
steering.autoNote: Note: 'auto' currently behaves like 'manual' — it's included only when you reference it by #name. Automatic selection is coming later.

form.name: Name
form.bodyPlaceholder: Write the guidance the AI should follow…

error.duplicate: A rule with this name already exists in this book.
error.cap: That exceeds a limit (body ≤ 8000 chars, name ≤ 200, ≤ 20 rules) or an invalid mode.
error.other: Couldn't save the rule. Please try again.
error.forbidden: You do not have permission to edit steering for this book.

palette.quickOpenHint: Type to search chapters, scenes & arcs

desc.toggleSidebar: Show or hide the side bar
desc.startGuidedTour: A quick tour of the core panels

manuscript.title: Manuscript
manuscript.body: Chapters & scenes — the navigator that drives the dock. Built next.

bible.title: Story Bible
bible.body: Cast, world & canon entries. Coming soon.

search.title: Search
search.body: Full-text & semantic search across the book. Coming soon.

quality.title: Quality
quality.body: Critic scores, promise threads & canon issues. Coming soon.

quality.noWork: This book has no co-writer session yet — set one up (or start composing a chapter), then quality data will appear here.
quality.hubNoWorkHint: Promises, critic scores, and story coverage need a co-writer session — set one up (or start composing a chapter). Canon issues below still work either way.
quality.setupWorkError: Could not set up the co-writer. Try again.
quality.setupWorkPending: Your co-writer session was created but its knowledge project is still being set up. Try again shortly.
quality.pickModel: Pick a model to analyze with…
quality.pickChapterHint: Pick a chapter above to analyze its quality.
quality.canonIntro: Advisory — confirmed contradictions with content marked gone/changed earlier in the book. Nothing here is applied automatically.
quality.canonCompositionError: Could not load canon issues from generation — try again.
quality.canonExtractionError: Could not load canon flags from knowledge extraction — try again.
quality.criticDesc: Per-chapter coherence, voice, pacing & canon scores
quality.canonRulesDesc: Author the invariants the critic enforces
quality.correctionsDesc: Your accept / edit / regenerate / reject rates
quality.healDesc: Run Polish and accept each fix before it touches your prose
quality.canonRuleError: Could not load canon-rule violations — try again.
quality.canonRuleGone: A rule that no longer exists
quality.canonRuleFocused: Showing violations of the rule you came from first ({{n}}): “{{rule}}”
quality.canonRuleFocusedEmpty: The rule you came from has no open violations. It is anchored here, but nothing has broken it.
quality.canonFocused: Showing the chapter you came from first ({{n}} finding(s)).
quality.canonFocusedEmpty: The chapter you came from has no canon findings here.
quality.canonUnavailable: Could not reach the co-writer service — generation-time canon findings are NOT included below.
quality.canonNoWork: This book has no co-writer session yet, so generation-time canon checks have not run. Only knowledge-extraction findings are shown.
quality.canonRuleFocusUnknown: The rule you came from could not be checked, because the co-writer service is unavailable. It may still be broken somewhere — this is not a result.
quality.canonRulesCapped: Showing the {{shown}} most recent of {{total}} broken rules.
quality.workUnavailable: Could not reach the co-writer service, so this book’s quality data could not be loaded. Problems may exist that are not shown here. Try again shortly.
quality.hubUnavailable: Could not reach the co-writer service, so promises, critic scores and story coverage cannot be loaded right now. Problems may exist that are not shown here.
quality.conformanceDesc: Did the prose realize the plan? Per-scene beat trace
quality.pickModelRerun: Pick a model to re-run with…
quality.pickChapterConformanceHint: Pick a chapter above to trace how its prose realized the plan.

layout.title: Panel layout
layout.needPanels: Open at least two panels first

bottomStub.jobs: Background jobs appear here once wired.
bottomStub.generation: Live generation progress appears here once wired.
bottomStub.issues: Quality issues appear here once wired.

manuscript.title: Manuscript
manuscript.restoreActHint: Restore this act (it comes back empty — re-file chapters as needed)
manuscript.actTrashed: Act "{{name}}" trashed — its chapters stayed in the book

intro.title: Welcome to Writing Studio
intro.subtitle: What are you here to do? This tailors your Welcome panel and quick tour — change it anytime from the Command Palette.

writer.title: Write a novel
writer.desc: Compose chapters with an AI co-writer, plan the system, edit the manuscript

worldbuilder.title: Build world & lore
worldbuilder.desc: Glossary entities, the wiki, and the knowledge graph

translator.title: Translate
translator.desc: Coverage matrix, bulk translate, per-chapter versions

enricher.title: Enrich existing text
enricher.desc: Find gaps, expand under-described entities, manage sources

manuscript.title: Your manuscript
manuscript.body: The Manuscript navigator drives the dock — pick a chapter or scene here to open it.

palette.title: Jump anywhere
palette.body: ⌘P jumps to a chapter/scene/arc; ⌘⇧P opens any Studio tool by name.

compose.title: Compose
compose.body: Your AI co-writer — draft, discuss, and apply prose changes here.

editor.title: Editor
editor.body: The manuscript editor — grammar, glossary hints, focus mode, and auto-save live here.

grammar.title: Grammar & spell check
grammar.body: Toggle live grammar and spelling checks on the current scene — powered by LanguageTool.

heatmap.title: Continuity heatmap
heatmap.body: Highlights lines that may drift from established canon, so you can spot-check them before publishing.

compose.title: Draft with your co-writer
compose.body: As a writer, this is where you'll spend a lot of time — ask for a first draft, a revision, or a sounding board for what happens next.

editor.title: Polish the prose
editor.body: Once a scene has words, refine it here — grammar checks, glossary hints, and focus mode all live in this editor.

grammar.title: Catch typos as you write
grammar.body: Toggle live grammar and spelling checks without leaving the page.

heatmap.title: Spot continuity drift
heatmap.body: Highlights lines that may contradict established canon — worth a glance before you move on.

planner.title: Plan the shape of the story
planner.body: Lay out arcs, chapters, and beats before or between writing sessions, so Compose and the Editor have a structure to write into.

glossary.title: Your world's dictionary
glossary.body: Every character, place, and item you've established lives here — search it, tag it, and keep it consistent.

wiki.title: Readable world articles
wiki.body: The wiki turns your glossary into narrative-style articles — a good way to review your world at a glance.

knowledge.title: The knowledge graph
knowledge.body: For deeper worldbuilding, the knowledge graph tracks how everything connects — entities, events, and relationships.

translation.title: Track translation coverage
translation.body: See which languages are covered, which chapters are stale, and kick off bulk translation runs from here.

enrichmentCompose.title: Fill in missing lore
enrichmentCompose.body: When a translation surfaces an under-described entity, use this panel to draft the missing lore before translating around it.

gaps.title: Find what's missing
gaps.body: This panel finds entities that are mentioned often but barely described, so you know exactly what to enrich next.

sources.title: Manage your grounding sources
sources.body: Enrichment drafts pull from the source material you manage here — keep it organized and license-tagged.

sharing.title: Control who sees this book
sharing.body: Set visibility, generate a share link, or invite collaborators — all from here.

bookSettings.title: Update the book's details
bookSettings.body: Title, cover, genre tags, and world link all live in Book Settings — update them any time.

grammar.title: Grammar & spell check
grammar.body: Toggle live grammar and spelling checks for the current scene.

heatmap.title: Continuity heatmap
heatmap.body: Highlights lines that may drift from established canon — worth a glance before you move on.

glossary.title: Glossary decoration
glossary.body: Toggles inline glossary hints on hover and the `[[` autocomplete while you write.

focus.title: Focus mode
focus.body: Dims everything but the current paragraph and hides the side panels — good for drafting without distraction.

scenes.title: Scene Rail
scenes.body: Shows this chapter's scenes — jump between them, reorder, or track status.

openJson.title: Open as JSON
openJson.body: Opens this same chapter as raw structured data — the same document, edits mirror live either way.

originalSource.title: Original Source
originalSource.body: A read-only view of the pre-translation source text, if this chapter was imported.

reader.title: Reader
reader.body: Opens this chapter in the reader view — how it looks to an actual reader.

translate.title: Translate
translate.body: Jumps straight to this chapter's translation versions — no need to go through the full Translation matrix.

save.title: Save
save.body: Saves your changes. ⌘S / Ctrl+S works from anywhere in the editor.

mode.title: Classic ⇄ AI mode
mode.body: Switch between a plain writing surface and one with AI tools available (selection Rewrite/Expand/Describe, continue-from-cursor).

continueStep.title: Continue writing with AI
continueStep.body: Streams an AI-generated continuation starting at your cursor. Select any text first to Rewrite, Expand, or Describe it instead.

checkpoints.title: AI-edit checkpoints
checkpoints.body: Every AI edit creates a restore point here — undo an AI change even after you've kept writing since.

revisionHistory.title: Revision history
revisionHistory.body: Every save is a version you can preview and restore, independent of AI checkpoints.

publish.title: Publish gate
publish.body: Publishing checks for unresolved canon contradictions and incomplete scenes first, so what readers see stays consistent.

rail.title: Scene Rail
rail.body: Lists this chapter's scenes — status, synopsis, and reordering all live here.

anchor.title: Anchor scenes
anchor.body: Matches scene titles to headings in the prose so the rail can jump straight to them.

add.title: Add a scene
add.body: Creates a new scene entry for this chapter.

toggle.title: Glossary
toggle.body: Highlights known entity names in your prose on hover, and typing `[[` opens an autocomplete to insert or link one. Turn it off here if it gets in the way while proofreading.

insert.title: Images
insert.body: Upload, drag-and-drop, or paste an image — or generate one with AI (you pick the model explicitly, never a silent default). Once added you can replace, resize, caption, add alt text, and browse its version history.

insert.title: Video
insert.body: Upload a video file, or generate a short clip with AI (explicit model picker). Replace, resize, caption, and version history all work the same as images.

insert.title: Audio
insert.body: Insert a standalone audio block — upload a file, or generate narration with AI. Tip: hovering ANY paragraph also shows a quick upload/record/AI-generate bar to attach narration directly to that text, no block needed.

textarea.title: Write to your co-writer
textarea.body: Enter sends, Shift+Enter for a new line. Ctrl+Enter forces a quick reply, Ctrl+Shift+Enter forces deeper thinking. Type / for templates and commands, @ to attach context inline.

permissionMode.title: Ask, Plan, or Write
permissionMode.body: Controls how much the agent can do on its own — Ask confirms everything, Plan lays out steps first, Write acts more freely within its allowlist. Ctrl+. cycles through them.

effort.title: Reasoning effort
effort.body: How hard the model thinks before replying — Off/Low/Medium/High/Auto. Hidden for models that can't reason.

attachContext.title: Attach context
attachContext.body: Pull in specific book, chapter, or glossary entries so the agent has exactly the context it needs for this message.

send.title: Send
send.body: Sends your message. While the agent is replying, this becomes a Stop button.

switcher.title: Switch conversations
switcher.body: Jump between this book's chat sessions, archive old ones, or start a new one — pick a model and a starting persona (Novelist/Translator/Worldbuilder/Editor).

rename.title: Rename this chat
rename.body: Give the session a name you'll recognize later.

settings.title: Session settings
settings.body: Model, composer/planner model overrides, memory grounding, system prompt, temperature, top-P, and reasoning effort — all scoped to this one session.

rack.title: Tools & skills rack
rack.body: Shows every tool and skill the agent can currently use in this chat — add more, or remove ones you don't want it reaching for.

add.title: Add tools or skills
add.body: Browse everything available by category and enable exactly what this conversation needs.

inspector.title: Agent runtime inspector
inspector.body: See what the agent is doing right now — discovering tools, running one, or idle — and expand for the full detail trail.

meter.title: Context budget
meter.body: Shows how much of the model's context window this conversation is using. Click it to see a full breakdown by category, and to compact the conversation on demand.

review.title: AI edits, reviewed before they land
review.body: When the agent proposes a prose edit, a review card appears here for you to accept or reject per change. Riskier actions (batch edits, destructive operations) get their own confirm card with a cost/impact preview first. Anything the agent auto-applies shows up in an Activity strip with a one-click Undo.

mode.title: Voice mode
mode.body: Talk instead of type — a full hands-free conversation with live transcription.

settings.title: Voice settings
settings.body: Pick your speech-to-text and text-to-speech models, voice, speed, and auto-send behavior.

popout.title: Pop out to its own window
popout.body: Opens Compose in a separate OS window — useful for a second monitor. Edits you approve there still land in this book's editor.

userGuide.intro: Every tool Writing Studio can open, grouped the same way as the Command Palette.

editorBasics.label: Editor Basics
editorBasics.desc: The toolbar strip — grammar, heatmap, glossary, focus, scenes, and quick-access buttons.

editorAiTools.label: AI Writing Tools
editorAiTools.desc: Continue-from-cursor, selection Rewrite/Expand/Describe, and the Classic/AI mode toggle.

editorDataSafety.label: Data Safety
editorDataSafety.desc: AI-edit checkpoints, revision history, and the publish gate.

editorSceneRail.label: Scene Rail
editorSceneRail.desc: Track scenes, reorder them, and keep the rail in sync with your prose.

editorGlossary.label: Glossary
editorGlossary.desc: Inline entity hints and the `[[` autocomplete while you write.

editorMediaImage.label: Media — Images
editorMediaImage.desc: Upload or AI-generate images, with replace/resize/caption/version-history.

editorMediaVideo.label: Media — Video
editorMediaVideo.desc: Upload or AI-generate short video clips.

editorMediaAudio.label: Media — Audio & Narration
editorMediaAudio.desc: Standalone audio blocks, plus per-paragraph narration you can upload, record, or AI-generate.

composerBasics.label: Composer Basics
composerBasics.desc: The message box, permission mode, reasoning effort, attach-context, and send.

composerSessions.label: Session Management
composerSessions.desc: Switch, rename, or start conversations, and open per-session settings.

composerAgentTools.label: Agent Tools & Skills
composerAgentTools.desc: See and manage which tools and skills the agent can currently reach for.

composerContextBudget.label: Context Budget
composerContextBudget.desc: How much of the model's context window this conversation is using, and how to compact it.

composerAiEditReview.label: AI Edit Review & Safety
composerAiEditReview.desc: How proposed edits, risky actions, and auto-applied changes get reviewed and can be undone.

composerVoice.label: Voice Assistant
composerVoice.desc: Talk instead of type, with configurable speech-to-text and text-to-speech.

composerPopout.label: Popout Window
composerPopout.desc: Pop Compose out into its own OS window for a second monitor.

empty.title: No plan for this book yet
empty.body: The Plan Hub shows the spec — arcs, chapters, scenes. This book has none yet. It will never be invented from your manuscript: extract it, or write one.
empty.extractCta: Extract the plan from the manuscript
empty.planCta: Paste a plan you've already written
empty.extractNote: Extracting reads the scenes already parsed from your chapters and turns each into a spec node. Grouping them into arcs is a separate AI step — ask the agent for it. Until then the extracted chapters sit in the “Unassigned” strip.
empty.nothingParsed: Nothing to extract — this book has no parsed scenes yet. Parse or import the chapters first, then extract.
empty.extractNeedsChapters: Extracting needs chapters — this book has none yet.

node.unplannedUnknown: Unplanned chapters: unknown (the manuscript could not be read)
node.unassignedStrip: {{count}} chapter in no arc — drag it into a lane
node.unassignedStrip_other: {{count}} chapters in no arc — drag them into a lane
node.unassignedFlow: {{count}} chapter not in any storyline yet
node.unassignedFlow_other: {{count}} chapters not in any storyline yet

toolbar.askAiHint: Select a node to ask about it

simple.guide: Your book, chapter by chapter. Click one to keep writing, or start a new chapter below.
simple.error: Couldn't load your chapters. Try again in a moment.
simple.emptyLine: A blank book. Start with a sentence — the structure can come later.
simple.doorNote: Opens a blank page straight away.
simple.aiDraft: Or let the AI draft one →

planHub.costHint: Editing structure makes no model calls.

flow.empty: No storylines yet — add an arc from the toolbar to begin.

adv.graphHint: Graph — zoom, pan and drag to move (best for a large structure)

structTpl.subtitle: story structures you decompose chapters against
structTpl.mineEmpty: None yet — clone a built-in above, or “New structure”.
structTpl.pickHint: Pick a structure to view its beats — or create a new one.
structTpl.builtinNote: This is a built-in structure. Clone it to customise — you edit your copy, the original stays intact for everyone.
structTpl.archivedNote: This structure is archived. Restore it to edit or use it again.
structTpl.discardBody: Your edits to this structure haven't been saved. Leaving now discards them.
structTpl.archiveBody: "{{name}}" moves to archived — you can restore it later.
structTpl.decomposeHint: To use this structure, open a chapter and run its Decompose step — your structures appear in the picker there.

err.conflict: This structure changed elsewhere — reload and reapply your edits.
err.duplicate: You already have a structure with that name.
err.unknown: Something went wrong — please try again.

rail.hint: Text finds exact words in your prose; Semantic finds passages by meaning. A hit opens the editor there.

semantic.noProject: This book has no knowledge project yet — extract or build knowledge first.
semantic.error: Semantic search is unavailable right now.
semantic.hint: Type at least 3 characters to search by meaning.
